title: Turla Turns Kazuar Backdoor Into Modular P2P Botnet for Persistent Access
description: The Russian state-sponsored hacking group known as        Turla      has transformed its custom backdoor Kazuar into a modular peer-to-peer (P2P) botnet that's engineered for stealth and persistent access to compromised hosts.   Turla, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), is assessed to be affiliated with Center 16 of Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB)
